# RAG-based Research Paper Q&A Agent (with MLOps layer)

Agentic RAG system over a small corpus of NLP/ML papers. Retrieval runs
over a FAISS index; a routing agent decides whether to **search**,
**summarize**, or **compare** papers; answers are generated by Claude and
grounded only in retrieved excerpts, with citations back to source papers.
MLflow logs experiment runs comparing chunking/embedding/retrieval configs.

## Architecture

```
data/papers/        -> drop your PDFs (or .txt) here
src/ingestion.py     -> chunk + embed + build FAISS index
src/rag_core.py       -> retrieve + Claude-generated cited answer
src/agent.py            -> router: search / summarize / compare
src/mlflow_experiments.py -> run configs, log params/metrics to MLflow
app.py                     -> Streamlit UI
```

## 1. Setup

```bash
python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate   # or use Docker, see below
pip install -r requirements.txt
cp .env.example .env    # add your ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
export $(cat .env | xargs)
```

**Before your real run:** replace the two placeholder files in
`data/papers/` (`Global_PIQA.txt`, `RAG_Survey.txt`) with your actual PDFs
(Global PIQA + related arXiv papers). Ingestion accepts both `.pdf` and
`.txt`, so you can test end-to-end with the placeholders right now.

## 2. Build the index

```bash
python -m src.ingestion --chunk-size 1000 --chunk-overlap 200 \
    --embedding-model sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2
```

## 3. Run the app

```bash
streamlit run app.py
```

Try queries like:
- "What dataset does Global PIQA use?" -> **search**
- "Summarize the RAG survey paper" -> **summarize**
- "Compare Global PIQA and the RAG survey" -> **compare**

## 4. MLflow experiments (chunk size / embedding model / retrieval-k tradeoffs)

```bash
python -m src.mlflow_experiments

```

This runs 4 configs (varying chunk size, embedding model, and k) and
logs ingestion time, chunk count, retrieval latency, and average
retrieval distance for each — real numbers you can point to.

## 5. Docker (whole app + MLflow server)

```bash
echo "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your_key_here" > .env
docker compose up --build
```

## 6. Deploy (optional, for a live link)

Push to GitHub, then deploy `app.py` on Streamlit Community Cloud or a
Hugging Face Space (Streamlit SDK). Add `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` as a secret
in the deployment platform's settings — never commit it.

## Design notes (for your write-up)

- **Chunking:** `RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter`, tunable chunk size /
  overlap — see MLflow runs for the size-vs-retrieval-quality tradeoff.
- **Embeddings:** local `sentence-transformers` models (no API cost,
  no rate limits) — swappable via `--embedding-model`.
- **Vector store:** FAISS, persisted locally to `data/faiss_index/`.
- **Agent:** a single router call classifies intent into
  search/summarize/compare before any retrieval happens — this is what
  makes the system agentic rather than a fixed retrieve-then-generate
  pipeline.
- **Grounding:** the answer prompt instructs Claude to answer only from
  retrieved excerpts and cite the source paper per claim, reducing
  hallucination risk.
